summary: The reference context is a placeholder — both spec entries are unfilled '?'. Two directly relevant specs exist (cclab-jet/pkg-manager.md at high, cclab-jet/pkg-manager-pnpm-parity.md at medium) but are not referenced. Additionally, the --no-install lock-only mode from Q2 has no spec coverage in either spec, and the existing pkg-manager.md specifies the metadata cache path as ~/.cache/jet/metadata/ (XDG) while the pre-clarification answer and requirements both specify ~/.jet-store/.metadata/ — a concrete spec inaccuracy that must be resolved before implementation.

checklist_results.item: All affected crates/areas from pre-clarifications are covered by at least one spec
checklist_results.note: Q2 requires a --no-install (lock-only) mode flag. Neither pkg-manager.md nor pkg-manager-pnpm-parity.md mentions this flag. pkg-manager.md covers the lockfile fast-path and frozen-lockfile (in parity spec) but not a mode that only writes the lockfile without downloading tarballs. This is a spec gap.

checklist_results.item: Relevance scores are reasonable (high = directly implements, medium = related, low = background)
checklist_results.note: Cannot evaluate — both entries are '?' placeholders. Based on actual spec content: pkg-manager.md deserves 'high' (directly implements metadata cache, HTTP/2, parallel downloads, BFS resolver — all core requirements for #881 and #883). pkg-manager-pnpm-parity.md deserves 'medium' (implements optionalDependencies platform filtering and npm: alias resolution, which are explicit resolver bug fixes in #883, but workspace/frozen-lockfile/audit features are out of scope).

checklist_results.item: Key requirements listed per spec are accurate (match actual requirement IDs)
checklist_results.note: Cannot evaluate — both entries have '— ' as key requirements. The actual specs use informal requirement descriptions rather than R-numbered IDs, so references should point to spec sections rather than requirement IDs.
Both placeholder entries are '?' — no concrete irrelevant specs are named. No false positives to flag.

issues.description: Reference context is entirely placeholder. Both rows have '? | ? | high | —' — the artifact CLI generated the template but no actual spec lookup was performed. The reference context provides zero guidance for implementation.
issues.recommendation: Replace placeholders with: (1) cclab-jet/pkg-manager.md at 'high' with key sections: registry metadata two-layer cache (x-caching annotation), HTTP/2 adaptive window, parallel download pipeline (Semaphore), BFS resolver with level-by-level prefetch, store layout. (2) cclab-jet/pkg-manager-pnpm-parity.md at 'medium' with key sections: optionalDependencies os/cpu/libc platform check, npm: alias resolver.

issues.description: Lock-only mode (--no-install flag from Q2) has no spec coverage. Pre-clarification Q2 explicitly requires a mode where jet resolves and writes jet-lock.yaml without downloading/extracting tarballs. Neither pkg-manager.md (which defines install paths) nor pkg-manager-pnpm-parity.md (which defines CLI flags) includes this flag or the corresponding install flow branch.
issues.recommendation: Before implementation, update pkg-manager.md to add: (1) --no-install flag in the /install OpenAPI path parameters; (2) a branch in the Install Flow diagram showing the resolution-only path that exits after writing jet-lock.yaml without executing the download/extract/hardlink phases.

issues.description: Cache path discrepancy between spec and requirements. pkg-manager.md specifies the L2 metadata cache at ~/.cache/jet/metadata/ (XDG standard), but pre-clarification Q1 answer and the requirements.md both specify ~/.jet-store/.metadata/ as the canonical path per issue #881.
issues.recommendation: Update pkg-manager.md registry API x-caching annotation and store-layout schema to use ~/.jet-store/.metadata/ before implementation. The XDG path (~/.cache/jet/metadata/) should be removed or noted as the migration source path only.
